admin_units: Mandatory Palestine (multiple districts, cities, and road corridors)
theater_description: Country-wide but highly uneven violence after the partition vote, with localized city, village, and road episodes rather than a single stable front.
geometry_method_note: Would require splitting into city, district, and corridor polygons; a whole-Palestine civilian denominator would overstate exposure for many sub-episodes.
military_area_note: Pre-state Jewish armed formations and Palestinian Arab militias/irregulars were not uniformly deployed across the territory at all times.
civilian_area_note: Exposure varied sharply by city, district, and road corridor; large-scale pre-state flight also changed local civilian presence over time.
split_reason: Belligerent structure and geography differ materially from the later interstate phase; a single war-wide denominator would be misleading.
quality_note: Mapped only in this bundle. Too heterogeneous for one denominator, and not fully reconstructed here.

episode_name: Jerusalem urban fighting and depopulation windows
geographic_scope: City-level: Jerusalem built-up area and immediate neighborhoods
theater_description: Urban fighting, siege conditions, neighborhood capture, and population flight inside Jerusalem's built-up area before and after the end of the Mandate.
geometry_method_note: Use the built-up city and immediate contested neighborhoods, not a whole-country denominator. British municipal boundaries were gerrymandered and need adjustment in later work.
military_area_note: Would need separate treatment of Haganah/IDF units, Arab Legion forces inside or adjacent to the city, and local armed formations.
civilian_area_note: Civilian presence changed sharply through siege, neighborhood flight, and the west/east split; municipal boundaries are not a clean denominator on their own.
split_reason: Jerusalem's city fighting and demographic change are distinct from the road corridor and from other fronts.
quality_note: Good geometry rationale exists, but the city-specific numerator and denominator series are not fully assembled here.

episode_name: Jerusalem corridor / Latrun-Bab al-Wad / Burma Road battles
geographic_scope: Corridor-level: Tel Aviv-Jerusalem road, Latrun salient, Bab al-Wad/Sha'ar Hagai, and Burma Road
theater_description: A moving road-corridor theater centered on convoy attacks, attempts to break the siege of Jerusalem, and repeated fighting around Latrun and alternative supply routes.
geometry_method_note: A corridor polygon following the main road and later the Burma Road is required; city or country totals would misstate exposure.
military_area_note: Relevant force exposure includes convoy escorts, corridor garrisons, Arab Legion units at Latrun, and units directly assigned to repeated assaults on the salient.
civilian_area_note: Civilian exposure should be limited to people present in corridor villages, roadside settlements, and convoy passengers, not all Jerusalem residents or all Israelis/Palestinians.
split_reason: Violence mode is logistical-corridor warfare rather than urban capture; the denominator footprint is linear and time-varying.
quality_note: Geometry is conceptually strong, but corridor population and repeated-assault casualty series were not fully reconstructed here.

episode_name: Lydda-Ramle urban capture and expulsion (Operation Dani stage 1)
geographic_scope: City-pair episode: Lydda (Lod) and Ramla built-up areas plus immediate urban approaches used in the assault
theater_description: Stage 1 of Operation Dani, covering the capture of the twin towns, the urban shootings inside Lydda, the seizure of Ramla, and the onset of the mass expulsion.
geometry_method_note: Use the two municipal/built-up towns and immediate assault approaches only. Exclude the later eastern village belt and Ramallah-al-Latrun highway fighting of Operation Dani stage 2.
military_area_note: Count only organized armed personnel directly assigned to the urban episode footprint; do not use whole-IDF or whole-Arab-state military totals.
civilian_area_note: Count only civilians physically present in the towns during 9-13 July, including refugee inflow from Jaffa and subtracting outflow beginning on 12 July and accelerating on 13 July.
split_reason: This is the strongest candidate for a defensible subnational denominator because sources describe who was present, who fled, and when.
quality_note: Pilot-only full estimate. Civilian exposure is reconstructable, but direct deaths cannot be split cleanly by status, so DR is left null.

episode_name: Ramallah-al-Latrun highway and eastern village belt (Operation Dani stage 2)
geographic_scope: Corridor/village episode east of Lydda-Ramle toward Bayt Nabala, al-Burj, Barfiliya, Salbit, and al-Latrun
theater_description: The second stage of Operation Dani as the offensive spilled eastward into the Jerusalem corridor and the Ramallah-al-Latrun highway villages.
geometry_method_note: Requires a village-and-road-corridor footprint distinct from the urban town pair.
military_area_note: Directly assigned Israeli assault units and Arab Legion/village defense forces need separate reconstruction; stage-1 urban denominators do not transfer.
civilian_area_note: Exposure depends on village-level depopulation and road-control timing, not on the urban populations of Lydda and Ramla.
split_reason: Different terrain, different civilian footprint, and different force deployment from the town capture.
quality_note: Probably estimable later, but not fully reconstructed here.

geographic_scope: Mixed multi-front interstate war (north, center, south, and cross-border sectors)
theater_description: Conventional and semi-conventional fighting after statehood across several fronts with distinct force structures and civilian footprints.
geometry_method_note: Must be split into front-specific polygons; a single national denominator would be analytically unsound.
military_area_note: Only theater-specific deployed forces should be counted in future work; national force totals are ceilings, not denominators.
civilian_area_note: Civilian exposure varied by front, town, and campaign, with large spatial differences in displacement and direct attack.
split_reason: The multi-front interstate phase is too large and heterogeneous for one episode-level DR estimate.
quality_note: Mapped only. Would require several separate front episodes rather than one aggregate denominator.
